explanations.3: This tells Docker to build an image by reading the Dockerfile in the current directory (the dot means 'here').
explanations.6: The first number is the port on your computer, the second is the port inside the container—so requests to your machine's port 80 get forwarded to the container's port 80.
explanations.9: This syncs a folder on your computer (./src) with a folder inside the container (/var/www/html/), so changes you make locally appear immediately in the running container.
